_meta.title: Deal Insight - Technical Specification
_meta.author: Stacey Shub
_meta.purpose: Complete scoring rubric, data model, and architecture spec for V1 build

signals.description: Six signals that determine deal health. Each is scored 0-100 on two dimensions: coverage (is the information there?) and depth (how strong is the evidence?).

list.name: The Pain
list.question: What problem are we solving and how badly does it hurt?

scoring.0-20: No pain identified, or generic ('they need better reporting')
scoring.20-40: Problem described but no specifics on who it affects or how
scoring.40-60: Problem is specific, affects named people/roles, but no cost attached
scoring.60-80: Problem is quantified (time lost, errors per month, manual hours). Consequence chain exists.
scoring.80-100: Dollar impact stated by prospect. Cost of inaction articulated. Multiple people have confirmed the pain independently.

coverage_checklist.item: Is a specific problem described?
Is it tied to a business process, not just a feeling?
Who does it affect? Named roles?
What is the consequence if they do not fix it?
Is there a dollar cost or time cost?
Has more than one person confirmed the pain?

list.coaching_prompt_example: You have described a problem but the prospect has not told you what it costs. Try: 'Do you have a rough sense of what this is costing the business annually?'

list.name: The Value
list.question: What is the economic or operational impact of solving this?

scoring.20-40: Generic value statement ('they want better visibility')
scoring.40-60: Desired future state described in prospect's words, but no dollar value
scoring.60-80: Dollar impact estimated. Solution is linked to specific outcomes.
scoring.80-100: Prospect has articulated the ROI themselves. Clear differentiator vs alternatives. Value ties to a specific person's success metrics.

coverage_checklist.item: Is the desired future state described?
Is it in the prospect's words, not the rep's pitch?
Is there a dollar value or measurable outcome?
Does the value connect to YOUR specific solution (not generic)?
Does a specific stakeholder own this outcome personally?
Would this make someone a hero internally?

list.coaching_prompt_example: You know their pain but have not connected it to a dollar outcome. Try: 'If we solved this completely, what would that free up for your team, and what is that worth?'

list.name: The People
list.question: Who makes the decision and who influences it?

scoring.0-20: One contact only, no understanding of decision structure
scoring.20-40: 2-3 contacts, but roles and influence unclear
scoring.40-60: Economic Buyer identified. Champion identified. But sentiment/influence not assessed.
scoring.60-80: Full map: EB, Champion, end users, and at least one potential blocker identified. You know what each person cares about individually.
scoring.80-100: Champion is tested and confirmed (they have taken action for you). EB has been engaged directly. Blocker strategy exists.

coverage_checklist.item: Is there more than one contact?
Is the Economic Buyer identified by name?
Is there a Champion?
Has the Champion done something for you (not just been friendly)?
Are blockers or skeptics identified?
Do you know what each stakeholder individually cares about?
Has the rep engaged at the executive level?

list.coaching_prompt_example: You have one contact and no executive access. Try: 'Who else on your team would want to see this before moving forward?' or 'If you brought this to your VP, what would their first question be?'

list.name: The Process
list.question: How will this purchase decision actually happen?

scoring.0-20: No understanding of how they buy
scoring.20-40: Vague sense ('I think the VP approves it')
scoring.40-60: Decision steps known but timeline and procurement details missing
scoring.60-80: Full process mapped: who approves, in what order, does legal review, what threshold triggers board involvement. Timeline estimated.
scoring.80-100: Process confirmed by prospect. Historical buying timeline referenced. Legal/procurement contacts identified. Mutual close plan exists with dates.

coverage_checklist.item: Are the decision steps documented?
Is the approval chain named (not just 'management')?
Do you know if legal/procurement gets involved?
Do you know the budget threshold for additional approvals?
Is there a realistic timeline based on how they have bought before?
Is there a mutual action plan with dates?

list.coaching_prompt_example: Your close date is next month but you do not know their signing process. Try: 'When you have purchased software like this before, what did the approval process look like, and how long did it take?'

list.name: The Competition
list.question: What alternatives exist and where do we stand?

scoring.0-20: Blank or 'no competition' (red flag, means you have not asked)
scoring.20-40: Competitors named but no detail on positioning
scoring.40-60: Competitors known. You know what the prospect likes about them.
scoring.60-80: Full picture: other vendors, internal build option, and status quo all assessed. You know your differentiated advantage for THIS deal.
scoring.80-100: Prospect has told you why they would choose you. Competitive positioning is tailored to their specific criteria. Status quo cost of inaction is articulated.

coverage_checklist.item: Are you evaluating other vendors? Named?
Is 'build it ourselves' on the table?
Is the status quo option addressed?
Do you know what they like about the alternatives?
Do you know YOUR specific advantage for THIS deal?
Has the prospect articulated why they would choose you?

list.coaching_prompt_example: Competition is blank. That usually means you have not asked, not that there is none. Try: 'Most companies in your space compare a few options. Where are you in that process?'

list.name: The Momentum
list.question: Is this deal moving forward or stalling?

scoring.0-20: No next step. No recent contact. Deal is dark.
scoring.20-40: Vague next step ('we will follow up'). Last contact was 2+ weeks ago.
scoring.40-60: Next step exists but no date. Or date exists but it is rep-initiated, prospect has not committed.
scoring.60-80: Specific next meeting scheduled. Prospect is initiating or co-driving. Close date has not moved.
scoring.80-100: Mutual action plan with milestones. Meeting cadence is accelerating. New stakeholders entering the conversation. Close date is stable or pulled in.

coverage_checklist.item: Is there a specific next step?
Is it on the calendar with a date?
Who initiated it, rep or prospect?
Has the close date moved? How many times?
Days since last meaningful contact?
Is meeting cadence increasing, steady, or decaying?
Are new stakeholders entering conversations?

list.coaching_prompt_example: No meeting scheduled and last contact was 18 days ago. This deal is drifting. Try: 'I want to respect your timeline. Is this still a priority for this quarter, or has something shifted?'

evidence_hierarchy.description: Four-level system for evaluating evidence quality. Applied as a multiplier to coverage score.

levels.label: Quantified Consequence
levels.description: Impact is measured but not in dollars

levels.label: Dollar Impact
levels.description: Money is attached to the impact

evidence_hierarchy.formula: Signal Score = Coverage (0-100) x Depth Multiplier (0.25 to 1.0)

deal_score.description: Composite 0-100 score from six weighted signal scores. Weights shift by deal stage.
deal_score.formula: Deal Score = sum(signal_score[i] * stage_weight[i]) for all 6 signals

v1_product.description: Minimal viable product. Proves the core intelligence engine.
workflow: Seller uploads a transcript or pastes notes
LLM extracts signal evidence from unstructured text
Evidence is structured into signal categories
Scoring engine evaluates signal strength and depth
System produces Deal Score and recommended next actions
outputs: Suggested next-step language per gap (2-3 options each)

assumed: string (things the rep believes but has not confirmed)

priority_gaps: reference to gaps sorted by severity

extracted_evidence.signal_id: string (pain | value | people | process | competition | momentum)
extracted_evidence.confidence: number (0-1, how confident the extraction is)

layers.name: Input Layer
layers.responsibility: Accepts transcript text, pasted notes, or email content
layers.tech_notes: Simple text input. V1 does not need file upload beyond paste. Future: Gong API, email API.

layers.name: Extraction Layer
layers.responsibility: LLM identifies signal evidence from unstructured text
layers.tech_notes: Prompt engineering with structured output. The LLM receives the text plus signal definitions and returns structured evidence per signal. JSON output mode recommended.

layers.name: Structuring Layer
layers.responsibility: Maps extracted evidence to signal categories and classifies evidence level
layers.tech_notes: May be combined with extraction layer in V1. Separate layer allows swapping extraction models without changing scoring.

layers.name: Scoring Engine
layers.responsibility: Evaluates coverage and depth per signal. Applies stage weights. Computes Deal Score.
layers.tech_notes: Deterministic logic, not LLM. Coverage from checklist matching. Depth from evidence level classification. Weights from stage_weights config.

layers.name: Coaching Engine
layers.responsibility: Identifies gaps and generates next-step language
layers.tech_notes: Gap identification is deterministic (low-scoring signals with specific checklist items missing). Coaching prompt selection can be template-based in V1, LLM-generated in V2.

layers.name: Output Layer
layers.responsibility: Assembles Deal Map, Deal Score, and Deal Focus into structured response
layers.tech_notes: JSON output for programmatic use. Rendered UI for human consumption.

layers.name: Interface
layers.responsibility: Simple web UI for upload and analysis
layers.tech_notes: V1: single page app. Text input area, analyze button, results panel. React recommended.

onboarding_intake: Sales process structure (stages, typical approval steps)
Average deal size and sales cycle length
Decision structure (who signs, budget ownership patterns)
Closed-won deal export (12-24 months with stage dates, contacts, deal size, cycle length)
Sales content (pitch deck, case studies, objection handling)
Tech stack (CRM, call recording, engagement tools)

continuous_calibration.description: As the system processes more deals and outcomes become known, the scoring model calibrates using historical data.
what_it_learns: Signals that strongly predict wins for this company
Signals commonly missing in lost deals
Signal progression patterns across successful deals
Individual rep scoring bias (future: per-rep calibration)

proof_tiers.minimum_viable_proof: 10-15 deals scored retroactively (mix of won, lost, stalled). Proves rubric separates winners from losers.
proof_tiers.investor_grade_proof: 30-50 deals across 2-3 reps or selling motions. Shows it works across different people and complexities.
proof_tiers.product_grade_proof: 100+ deals from a design partner. Full statistical validation.

brand.name: Deal Insight

teal.role: Primary product color. UI, buttons, links, active states.

purple.role: Signal: Critical insight. High-impact deal signal.

brand.rule: Signal colors never decorate. They always communicate.
